# meta-toolkit

> A zero-dependency JavaScript toolkit for meta programming and OOP: name-casing conversions, property descriptor manipulation, aliases, prototype traversal, iterator helpers, deep path access, option merging, and comparator adapters.

- NPM: https://npmjs.org/package/meta-toolkit
- GitHub: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit
- Wiki: https://github.com/uhop/meta-toolkit/wiki
- License: BSD-3-Clause
- Runtime: Node.js, Bun, Deno, browsers
- Module system: ESM (`"type": "module"`)
- TypeScript: hand-written `.d.ts` declarations for all modules

## Installation

```bash
npm install meta-toolkit
```

## Quick start

```js
import {fromKebabCase, toCamelCase, toPascalCase, toSnakeCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';

const parts = fromKebabCase('foo-bar-baz');
console.log(toCamelCase(parts));  // 'fooBarBaz'
console.log(toPascalCase(parts)); // 'FooBarBaz'
console.log(toSnakeCase(parts));  // 'foo_bar_baz'
```

```js
import {addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';

class Foo {
  constructor() { this.value = 2; }
  get double() { return this.value * 2; }
  line(a, b) { return a * this.value + b; }
}
addAliases(Foo.prototype, {
  double: 'x2, duplicate',
  line: 'linear'
});

const f = new Foo();
console.log(f.x2);         // 4
console.log(f.linear(1, 2)); // 4
```

```js
import {get, set, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';

const obj = {};
forceSet(obj, 'a.b.c', 1); // obj = {a: {b: {c: 1}}}
get(obj, 'a.b.c');          // 1
set(obj, 'a.b.c', 2);      // obj = {a: {b: {c: 2}}}
remove(obj, 'a.b.c');       // obj = {a: {b: {}}}
```

---

## Module: Names (`meta-toolkit/names.js`)

Name-casing conversions. All `from*` functions split a string into `string[]`. All `to*` functions join `string[]` into a string.

```js
import {capitalize, fromCamelCase, toCamelCase, fromPascalCase, toPascalCase,
        fromSnakeCase, toSnakeCase, toAllCapsSnakeCase,
        fromKebabCase, toKebabCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';
```

### Functions

- `capitalize(name: string): string` ŌĆö capitalize first letter, lowercase rest.
- `fromCamelCase(name: string): string[]` ŌĆö split camelCase into parts.
- `toCamelCase(names: string[]): string` ŌĆö join parts as camelCase.
- `fromPascalCase(name: string): string[]` ŌĆö split PascalCase into parts.
- `toPascalCase(names: string[]): string` ŌĆö join parts as PascalCase.
- `fromSnakeCase(name: string): string[]` ŌĆö split snake_case into parts.
- `toSnakeCase(names: string[]): string` ŌĆö join parts as snake_case.
- `toAllCapsSnakeCase(names: string[]): string` ŌĆö join parts as UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
- `fromKebabCase(name: string): string[]` ŌĆö split kebab-case into parts.
- `toKebabCase(names: string[]): string` ŌĆö join parts as kebab-case.

### Example

```js
import {fromCamelCase, toKebabCase, toSnakeCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';

const parts = fromCamelCase('fooBarBaz');
// parts = ['foo', 'Bar', 'Baz']

toKebabCase(parts);  // 'foo-bar-baz'
toSnakeCase(parts);  // 'foo_bar_baz' ŌĆö note: parts are lowercased by toSnakeCase
```

---

## Module: Descriptors (`meta-toolkit/descriptors.js`)

Property descriptor creation and manipulation helpers.

```js
import {defaultDescriptor, makeGetter, makeSetter, makeAccessors,
        addDescriptor, addDescriptors, addAccessor, addGetters,
        copyDescriptors} from 'meta-toolkit/descriptors.js';
```

### Constants

- `defaultDescriptor` ŌĆö `{configurable: true, enumerable: false}`. Used as the default `initDescriptor` for descriptor factories.

### Descriptor factories

- `makeGetter(getter: () => any, initDescriptor?: PropertyDescriptor): PropertyDescriptor` ŌĆö create a getter descriptor.
- `makeSetter(setter: (value: any) => void, initDescriptor?: PropertyDescriptor): PropertyDescriptor` ŌĆö create a setter descriptor.
- `makeAccessors(getter: () => any, setter: (value: any) => void, initDescriptor?: PropertyDescriptor): PropertyDescriptor` ŌĆö create a getter+setter descriptor.

### Adding descriptors to objects

- `addDescriptor(target: object, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], descriptor: PropertyDescriptor, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö define a descriptor on target under one or more names. `names` can be a comma-separated string. Skips existing properties unless `force` is truthy.
- `addDescriptors(target: object, dict: DescriptorDict, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö define multiple descriptors from a dictionary. Keys are comma-separated name strings or symbols; values are descriptors. Supports symbol keys.
- `addAccessor(target: object, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], getter: () => any, setter: (value: any) => void, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö shorthand for `addDescriptor` + `makeAccessors`.
- `addGetter(target: object, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], getter: () => any, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö singular counterpart of `addGetters`. Adds a single getter under one or more names.
- `addGetters(target: object, dict: GetterDict, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö add multiple getters from a dictionary. Keys are comma-separated name strings; values are getter functions.

### Class-prototype-aware sugar

- `addProtoGetter(Class: {prototype: object}, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], getter: () => any, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö equivalent to `addGetter(Class.prototype, names, getter, force)`. Returns the prototype.
- `addProtoGetters(Class: {prototype: object}, dict: GetterDict, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö equivalent to `addGetters(Class.prototype, dict, force)`. Returns the prototype.

Use these when the call site is targeting a class's prototype, so the code reads `Foo` instead of `Foo.prototype`.

### Copying descriptors

- `copyDescriptors(target: object, source: object, names: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[] | AliasDict, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö copy descriptors from source to target. `names` can be:
  - A string (comma-separated) or symbol or array ŌĆö copies those descriptors as-is.
  - An `AliasDict` object ŌĆö keys are source property names, values are target alias names.

### Types

```ts
interface DescriptorDict {
  [name: string | symbol]: PropertyDescriptor;
}
interface GetterDict {
  [name: string | symbol]: () => any;
}
interface AliasDict {
  [name: string | symbol]: string | symbol | (string | symbol)[];
}
```

### Example

```js
import {makeGetter, addDescriptor, addGetters} from 'meta-toolkit/descriptors.js';

const obj = {_x: 10};
addDescriptor(obj, 'x, getX', makeGetter(function() { return this._x; }));
console.log(obj.x);    // 10
console.log(obj.getX);  // 10

addGetters(obj, {
  'doubled, x2': function() { return this._x * 2; }
});
console.log(obj.doubled); // 20
console.log(obj.x2);      // 20
```

---

## Module: Aliases (`meta-toolkit/aliases.js`)

Property aliasing built on `copyDescriptors` and `addDescriptor` from `descriptors.js`.

```js
import {addAlias, addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';
```

### Functions

- `addAlias(object: object, name: string | symbol, aliases: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö alias a single property. Copies the property descriptor from `name` to each alias.
- `addAliases(object: object, dict: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[] | AliasDict, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö alias multiple properties. Dict keys are source property names; values are alias names (comma-separated string, symbol, or array).

### Class-prototype-aware sugar

- `addProtoAlias(Class: {prototype: object}, name: string | symbol, aliases: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[], force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö equivalent to `addAlias(Class.prototype, name, aliases, force)`. Returns the prototype.
- `addProtoAliases(Class: {prototype: object}, dict: string | symbol | (string|symbol)[] | AliasDict, force?: boolean): object` ŌĆö equivalent to `addAliases(Class.prototype, dict, force)`. Returns the prototype.

### Example

```js
import {addAlias, addAliases, addProtoAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';

class Queue {
  enqueue(value) { /* ... */ }
  dequeue() { /* ... */ }
}

addAlias(Queue.prototype, 'enqueue', 'push, add');
addAliases(Queue.prototype, {dequeue: 'pop, remove'});

// Same with prototype-aware sugar (no `.prototype` at the call site):
class Stack {
  push(value) { /* ... */ }
  pop() { /* ... */ }
}
addProtoAliases(Stack, {push: 'add, enqueue', pop: 'remove, dequeue'});

// Now Queue and Stack instances have synonymous methods.
```

---

## Module: Iterators (`meta-toolkit/iterators.js`)

Iterator augmentation and lazy transformation helpers.

```js
import {augmentIterator, normalizeIterator, mapIterator, filterIterator} from 'meta-toolkit/iterators.js';
```

### Functions

- `augmentIterator<T>(iterator: Iterator<T>): IterableIterator<T>` ŌĆö add `[Symbol.iterator]` returning `this` if not already present.
- `normalizeIterator<T>(iterator: Iterator<T>): IterableIterator<T>` ŌĆö uses `Iterator.from()` if available (modern runtimes), otherwise falls back to `augmentIterator`.
- `mapIterator<T, R>(iterator: Iterable<T>, callbackFn: (value: T, index: number) => R): Iterable<R>` ŌĆö lazy map. Uses native `iterator.map()` if available.
- `filterIterator<T>(iterator: Iterable<T>, callbackFn: (value: T, index: number) => boolean): Iterable<T>` ŌĆö lazy filter. Uses native `iterator.filter()` if available.

### Example

```js
import {mapIterator, filterIterator} from 'meta-toolkit/iterators.js';

function* range(n) { for (let i = 0; i < n; ++i) yield i; }

const doubled = mapIterator(range(5), x => x * 2);
const evens = filterIterator(range(10), x => x % 2 === 0);

for (const v of doubled) console.log(v); // 0, 2, 4, 6, 8
for (const v of evens) console.log(v);   // 0, 2, 4, 6, 8
```

---

## Module: Prototypes (`meta-toolkit/prototypes.js`)

Prototype chain inspection utilities.

```js
import {prototypes, getPropertyDescriptor} from 'meta-toolkit/prototypes.js';
```

### Functions

- `prototypes(object: object, skipSelf?: boolean): Generator<object>` ŌĆö generator yielding each prototype in the chain, stopping before `Object.prototype`. If `skipSelf` is truthy, skips the first (own) object.
- `getPropertyDescriptor(object: object, name: string | symbol): PropertyDescriptor | undefined` ŌĆö like `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor` but walks the entire prototype chain.

### Example

```js
import {prototypes, getPropertyDescriptor} from 'meta-toolkit/prototypes.js';

class A { foo() {} }
class B extends A { bar() {} }

for (const proto of prototypes(new B())) {
  console.log(Object.getOwnPropertyNames(proto));
}
// []
// ['constructor', 'bar']
// ['constructor', 'foo']

const desc = getPropertyDescriptor(new B(), 'foo');
console.log(typeof desc.value); // 'function'
```

---

## Module: Path (`meta-toolkit/path.js`)

Deep property access by dot-delimited string path or array of keys.

```js
import {get, set, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';
```

### PathOptions

```ts
interface PathOptions {
  delimiter?: string | RegExp;  // Default: '.'
  defaultValue?: any;
}
```

### Functions

- `get(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], options?: PathOptions): any` ŌĆö get a nested value. Returns `options.defaultValue` if the path does not exist.
- `set(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], value: any, options?: PathOptions): any` ŌĆö set a nested value. The path must already exist (intermediate objects are not created). Returns the previous value, or `options.defaultValue` if the path did not exist.
- `forceSet(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], value: any, options?: PathOptions): any` ŌĆö set a nested value, creating intermediate empty objects `{}` as needed. Throws `TypeError` if the root object is not dereferenceable or the path is empty.
- `remove(object: object, path: string | (string|symbol)[], options?: PathOptions): any` ŌĆö delete a nested property. Returns the removed value, or `options.defaultValue` if the path did not exist.

### Path safety

These functions walk user-supplied keys without sanitizing magic property names (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`). If your application passes externally-sourced paths (HTTP request bodies, configuration files, or other untrusted input) directly to these functions, validate them at the application boundary first ŌĆö the library does not. The trust boundary is the caller; library users are responsible for input sanitization.

### Example

```js
import {get, set, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';

const config = {};
forceSet(config, 'db.host', 'localhost');
forceSet(config, 'db.port', 5432);
// config = {db: {host: 'localhost', port: 5432}}

get(config, 'db.host');              // 'localhost'
get(config, 'db.user', {defaultValue: 'root'}); // 'root'

set(config, 'db.port', 3306);
remove(config, 'db.host');
// config = {db: {port: 3306}}
```

---

## Module: Options (`meta-toolkit/options.js`)

Constructor option merging utility.

```js
import {copyOptions} from 'meta-toolkit/options.js';
```

### Functions

- `copyOptions(target: object, defaults: object, ...sources: object[]): object` ŌĆö merge defaults then sources into target. If `target` is null/undefined, a new object is created. First, all non-undefined values from `defaults` are copied to `target`. Then, for each source, only keys that exist in `defaults` are copied. Returns `target`.

### Example

```js
import {copyOptions} from 'meta-toolkit/options.js';

const defaults = {color: 'red', size: 10, visible: true};
const userOpts = {size: 20, extra: 'ignored'};

const result = copyOptions({}, defaults, userOpts);
// result = {color: 'red', size: 20, visible: true}
// 'extra' is ignored because it's not in defaults
```

---

## Module: Comparators (`meta-toolkit/comparators.js`)

Adapters between `less` (`(a, b) => boolean`), `compare` (`(a, b) => number`), and `equal` function styles.

```js
import {compareFromLess, lessFromCompare, equalFromLess,
        reverseLess, reverseCompare} from 'meta-toolkit/comparators.js';
```

### Functions

- `compareFromLess(lessFn: (a, b) => boolean): (a, b) => number` ŌĆö adapt a less function to a compare function. Returns -1, 0, or 1.
- `lessFromCompare(compareFn: (a, b) => number): (a, b) => boolean` ŌĆö adapt a compare function to a less function.
- `equalFromLess(lessFn: (a, b) => boolean): (a, b) => boolean` ŌĆö derive equality: returns true if neither `less(a,b)` nor `less(b,a)`.
- `reverseLess(lessFn: (a, b) => boolean): (a, b) => boolean` ŌĆö reverse a less function (swaps arguments).
- `reverseCompare(compareFn: (a, b) => number): (a, b) => number` ŌĆö reverse a compare function (swaps arguments).

### Example

```js
import {compareFromLess, reverseLess} from 'meta-toolkit/comparators.js';

const less = (a, b) => a < b;
const compare = compareFromLess(less);

console.log(compare(1, 2));  // -1
console.log(compare(2, 1));  //  1
console.log(compare(1, 1));  //  0

const descLess = reverseLess(less);
console.log(descLess(1, 2)); // false
console.log(descLess(2, 1)); // true

[3, 1, 2].sort(compare);                        // [1, 2, 3]
[3, 1, 2].sort(compareFromLess(descLess));       // [3, 2, 1]
```

---

## Common patterns

### Name conversion pipeline

```js
import {fromCamelCase, toKebabCase, toPascalCase, toSnakeCase} from 'meta-toolkit/names.js';

const parts = fromCamelCase('myVariableName');
console.log(toKebabCase(parts));   // 'my-variable-name'
console.log(toPascalCase(parts));  // 'MyVariableName'
console.log(toSnakeCase(parts));   // 'my_variable_name'
```

### Dynamic accessor generation

```js
import {addAccessor} from 'meta-toolkit/descriptors.js';

class Point {
  constructor(x, y) { this._x = x; this._y = y; }
}

addAccessor(Point.prototype, 'x',
  function() { return this._x; },
  function(v) { this._x = v; }
);

const p = new Point(1, 2);
console.log(p.x); // 1
p.x = 10;
console.log(p.x); // 10
```

### Aliasing methods for API compatibility

```js
import {addAliases} from 'meta-toolkit/aliases.js';

class Collection {
  pushBack(value) { /* ... */ }
  popFront() { /* ... */ }
  getLength() { /* ... */ }
}

addAliases(Collection.prototype, {
  pushBack: 'push, add, enqueue',
  popFront: 'pop, remove, dequeue',
  getLength: 'size, length'
});
```

### Lazy iterator chaining

```js
import {mapIterator, filterIterator} from 'meta-toolkit/iterators.js';

function* integers() { for (let i = 0; ; ++i) yield i; }

// Lazy: nothing computed until iterated
const pipeline = mapIterator(
  filterIterator(integers(), n => n % 3 === 0),
  n => n * n
);

// Take first 5
let count = 0;
for (const v of pipeline) {
  console.log(v); // 0, 9, 36, 81, 144
  if (++count >= 5) break;
}
```

### Deep configuration management

```js
import {get, forceSet, remove} from 'meta-toolkit/path.js';

const config = {};
forceSet(config, 'server.host', '0.0.0.0');
forceSet(config, 'server.port', 8080);
forceSet(config, 'db.connection.host', 'localhost');
forceSet(config, 'db.connection.port', 5432);

console.log(get(config, 'server'));
// {host: '0.0.0.0', port: 8080}

remove(config, 'db.connection.port');
console.log(get(config, 'db.connection'));
// {host: 'localhost'}
```
